title: Urgent: Geo-traffic shift request — Ridgeway production latency and 5xx spike
preview:
From: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwo od.com>
Cc: sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:13:00 -0800
Subject: Production latency and 5xx spike — need geo shift

Alex —

We're seeing a sudden 6-8x latency increase for critical inference routes and a rising 5xx rate beginning ~08:45 PST. Error rates are concentrated in us-west-2, AZ b. Throughput is fine but token latencies are killing our SLAs and clients are opening tickets. Can Redwood initiate an emergency geo routing shift to us-east-1 (read traffic at minimum) while Ridgeway SRE collects logs?

Quick details:
- Impact: 30% of requests seeing >2s p50, 8% 5xx (gateway timeouts)
- Seen across multiple clients, started 08:45 PST
- Oncall has checked our infra; looks like Redwood-backed endpoints are returning 502s and timeouts

We need a runbook-driven mitigation and an ETA. Please escalate to engineering and exec if needed.

Attaching: none (we can upload logs on the call).

Jessica White
Head of Platform Ops | Ridgeway Analytics


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — need geo shift

Jessica — thanks for the heads up and sorry for the disruption. I'm pulling the on-call infra and our routing team in now. Can you confirm whether clients are session-pinned (no sticky routing) for the impacted endpoints and whether any customer-specific routing policies are in place?

We propose an immediate, partial traffic diversion to us-east-1 for non-sticky read-only inference routes while we stabilize us-west-2. I'll loop Rishi for a quick diagnosis and proposed commands.

Standby for a call invite (10m).

Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_runbook_failover.pdf (runbook excerpt)


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:30:00 -08:00
Subject: FYI / Triage: Ridgeway production — propose geo shift to us-east-1

Team —
Ridgeway just reported a large latency/5xx spike affecting us-west-2 (AZ b). They asked for an emergency geo shift for critical inference routes. I'm on a quick call with customer.

Immediate asks:
- Rishi: quick triage (KV cache, node saturation, GPU queueing) and proposed mitigations with exact CLI/console steps.
- Evelyn: prepare a rollback plan and check our cross-region capacity (us-east-1 burst availability).
- Marissa: I'm going to need a short exec note and signoff on any credit/comp if this extends past 4 hours.

Attached runbook (excerpt) — follow the \"traffic diversion\" section; we should prefer prefix-based routing to minimize cache misses.

On the call now.

Alex
(On behalf of Ridgeway escalation)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 10:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: FYI / Triage: Ridgeway production — propose geo shift to us-east-1

Quick findings from our initial probes (10:00–10:03 PST):
- us-west-2 fleet nodes show elevated GPU queuing and intermittent gRPC timeouts to the KV cache layer. Token generation latency spikes align with cache miss storms between 08:40–09:05 PST.
- No obvious host CPU/memory OOMs; autoscaler did not fire because node-local sched pressure prevented new pods from accepting traffic.

Immediate mitigation (low-risk) I recommend now:
1) Prefix-based diversion: shift 30–40% of non-sticky traffic for route set /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 via the routing policy UI or our routing-api: POST /routing/shift {\"from\":\"us-west-2\",\"to\":\"us-east-1\",\"percent\":35,\"routes\":[\"/api/v1/infer/read/*\"]}
2) Evict aged KV entries: run kvctl flush --prefix temp-cache-20270212 on affected clusters (this reduces cache-miss amplification)
3) Increase worker concurrency on us-west-2 nodes by +2 to drain queues (risk: temporary increased latency at tail)

I attached a short debug dump I asked Ridgeway to upload. Actions are safe to run now; expected customer p50 improvement within 5–8 minutes and drop in 5xx from 8% to ~1.5% as load shifts.

Attachments: ridgeway_debug_packets_0212.txt (extract), traffic-shift_proposal.pdf

-Rishi
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 12:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — Ridgeway mitigation in flight

Jessica / Ridgeway SRE —

We executed a 35% prefix diversion for /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 at 11:53 PST and evicted aged KV entries as per Rishi's recommendation. Early telemetry:
- p50 dropped from ~1.8s to ~380ms for diverted traffic
- 5xx rate dropped from 8% to 1.7% fleet-wide
- us-west-2 still experiencing higher tail latency for sticky write routes; we did not divert write/sensitive routes.

We're continuing to monitor and will keep the diversion in place until we finish a controlled drain and KV warmup on us-west-2. Please flag if you observe any client regressions (auth, session consistency).

Thanks for the quick coordination.

Alex

> 2) Evict aged KV entries: run kvctl flush --prefix temp-cache-20270212 on affected clusters (this reduces cache-miss amplification)
> 3) Increase worker concurrency on us-west-2 nodes by +2 to drain queues (risk: temporary increased latency at tail)



From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 18:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Ridgeway outage: summary, next steps, and follow-up

Jessica / Ridgeway leadership —

Thanks again for your patience and for working through the mitigation with our team today. Below is a concise summary and next steps: 

Summary:
- Incident window: 2027-02-12 08:40–12:00 PST (mitigation began 11:53 PST)
- Root cause (initial): KV cache miss amplification combined with node-level scheduling pressure in us-west-2 AZ b leading to GPU queueing and gRPC timeouts.
- Mitigation performed: 35% prefix traffic diversion to us-east-1 for read-only inference routes, KV eviction, temporary concurrency increase.
- Current status: customer traffic stable; total 5xx reduced to <2% and p50 restored for diverted traffic.

Immediate next steps:
1) Joint postmortem: we propose a 60-minute bridge on Monday 2027-02-15 to review full traces, autotuning, and routing policy thresholds. Link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3Cpostmortem
2) Action items (assigned): Rishi to run kernel-level profiling and propose autoscaler threshold updates (JIRA SUP-4872). Evelyn to validate cross-region capacity and update our burst pool config. Alex to own customer comms and credit proposal.
3) Credit: we will issue a goodwill credit for the customer SLA window; Alex to follow up with details (HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/8271).

We will draft the full postmortem and share before the Monday call. If you need anything sooner, mark the bridge or ping me directly.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
Attachments: traffic-shift_proposal.pdf (internal remediation notes)

title: Usage pricing and premium model multipliers for TideWave pilot
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@tidewave.ai>
Date: 2027-02-10T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Usage pricing and premium model multipliers for TideWave pilot

Hi Elena,

Great talking earlier—thanks for outlining the pilot goals. Per our discussion I put together an initial usage structure for hosted API pricing so you can see how commit bands and model premiums impact unit economics. Attached: TW_Commit_Tier_Pricing.xlsx (slab examples).

High level (per-token generation):
- Standard chat model (baseline): $0.0006 per token
- Standard embeddings: $0.00015 per token

Commit tier discounts (monthly committed tokens):
- 0 – 1M tokens: baseline pricing (no discount)
- 1M – 10M: 15% discount off baseline
- 10M – 100M: 30% discount off baseline
- 100M+: custom (we can build a blended schedule)

Model-specific premium multipliers (applies to model-class selection):
- Premium chat models (higher capacity / quality): 2.8x baseline token price
- Premium embeddings: 1.9x baseline token price

Notes / questions for you:
1) Do you expect the pilot to be embedding-heavy (vector indexing) or chat-heavy (user-facing generation)?
2) Is TideWave open to a committed monthly spend (examples in the attachment) or do you need an on-demand-only comparison?
3) Any procurement constraints (PO process, SOC2 evidence) we should plan for now?

If helpful we can propose a capped multiplier for the pilot or a blended SKU to simplify billing—happy to loop in pricing.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben@redwoodinference.com | (415) 555-0198

Attachment: TW_Commit_Tier_Pricing.xlsx (spreadsheet, 2 sheets: \"Slabs\" and \"Examples\")


From: Elena Morales <elena@tidewave.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@tidewave.ai
Date: 2027-02-10T10:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Usage pricing and premium model multipliers for TideWave pilot

Ben,

Thanks—this is a helpful start. A few clarifications from our side:
- Our pilot will be roughly 70% embeddings (semantic search) and 30% chat (prompted responses).
- We were hoping to avoid a large premium multiplier: 2.8x on chat would materialize into a big jump for user-visible features. Is there room to cap the multiplier or offer a blended rate for the pilot?
- Procurement wants to understand minimum commit thresholds and whether we can do a 3-month ramp (e.g., 20% / 40% / 40% of committed volume).
- We also need a brief statement of your SOC 2 status and typical API latency SLOs for hosted customers.

Cc-ing procurement so they have the thread.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Product | TideWave AI
elena@tidewave.ai

Proposed pilot terms (90 days):
- Committed monthly spend: $40,000 (can be invoiced monthly or prepaid).
- Ramp schedule: Month 1 = 25% of committed token volume; Month 2 = 50%; Month 3 = 100% (we prorate discounts per month).
- Discounting: baseline slabs as Ben proposed, but applied to committed spend rather than raw token counts to simplify billing for procurement.

Premium model treatment (pilot concession):
- Capped premium multiplier for pilot: 1.8x for chat (instead of 2.8x).
- Premium embeddings: capped at 1.4x for pilot.
- If TideWave converts to an annual committed contract, we will re-evaluate and can reduce multipliers further based on committed volume.

Additional items: 
- Blended SKU option: we can provide a blended per-token rate for the pilot that weights embeddings/chat according to your stated 70/30 split. That yields an illustrative blended rate of $0.00035 / token (after pilot caps and discounts)—we'll show exact numbers in the attached spreadsheet.
- SOC 2: Redwood is SOC 2 Type II in scope; see the FAQ in the SLA draft.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile under typical production load: <220ms for embeddings, <450ms for short-chat (this is region and sequence-length dependent; we include trace examples).
- Acceptance: signature via DocuSign link below; procurement can submit PO after execution.

DocuSign: https://docusign.com/TW-2027-42-offer

title: Grafana/Datadog connector - proxy & auth handshake
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Milton <sara.milton@grafana.com>, partner-ops@datadog.com
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Grafana/Datadog connector - proxy & auth handshake

Hi Sara / Datadog Partner Ops,

Quick intro: Redwood is building a managed connector that allows Grafana Agent -> Redwood runtime -> Datadog exporter in customer VPCs (common ask for regulated customers). A few customers hit a blocker around corporate proxy deployments and mutual auth between the agent and the exporter.

We'd like to align on supported auth modes and recommended proxy patterns before we finalize the marketplace package. Specific asks:
- Which proxy auth flows do you recommend for Grafana Agent when the outbound is to an internal Datadog collector (mTLS, token passthrough, or OAuth delegation)?
- Are there Datadog-supported intermediary proxy configurations that preserve trace/span integrity and headers?
- Any known gotchas for air-gapped or egress-restricted environments (CA bundles, hostname verification)?

I've attached a scribble of the proposed flow (auth_flow_diagram.png) and a draft mutual action plan (mutual-action-plan.xlsx). Can we get a short technical sync (30m) this week with the infra owners so we can lock the connector auth matrix?

Thanks — cc'ing Laura (platform integrator) and Neha (security/compliance).

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Partnerships | Redwood Inference
+1 415-555-0132

Attachments: auth_flow_diagram.png (image), mutual-action-plan.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Sara Milton <sara.milton@grafana.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim Huang <tim.huang@datadoghq.com>, partner-ops@datadog.com
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 13:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Grafana/Datadog connector - proxy & auth handshake

Thanks Ben — good context.

Short answers up front:
- Datadog export can be configured for token-based auth or mTLS. mTLS is preferred for zero-trust setups; token passthrough works for customers that centralize creds in the agent.
- For proxies we generally recommend a transparent TLS-forwarding proxy that preserves SNI and client certs. If the proxy terminates TLS, you must re-establish mTLS on the proxy->collector leg — that is a common source of header loss.
- Air-gapped customers: ensure CA bundles include Datadog collector CAs and validate hostname overrides if using internal DNS. We often see failures when proxies rewrite Host headers.

Two asks from our side:
1) Can you share a minimal config snippet for Grafana Agent in the proposed flow? That will help our engineering team reproduce the path.
2) If you have a list of customers that need the air-gap pattern, mark them in the M-A-P so we can prioritize packaging.

I can do a 30m sync Thurs 11am PT. Looping in Tim from Datadog to confirm collector behavior in proxied setups.

—Sara
Sara Milton
Partner Solutions Engineer, Observability Integrations
Grafana Labs

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>

Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Grafana/Datadog connector - proxy & auth handshake

Forwarding Sara's reply and Tim's inclusion. Laura — can you pick this up as the technical owner? Key items to drive in the sync:
- Minimal Grafana Agent config snippets for mTLS and token modes (Sara asked for this).
- Validation tests we can run in our staging: header passthrough, CA bundle injection, hostname override.
- If we choose mTLS, outline automated cert rotation approach and key rollout path for marketplace artifacts.

Neha — flagging potential compliance questions for air-gapped customers (key escrow, CA pinning). Jordan — please scope the change to the connector packaging (config templates + install script).

Suggested sync: Thu 11am PT with Sara + Tim + us. I'll send calendar invite once you confirm.

Thanks,
Ben

title: QBR compressed flightcheck: readiness, launch windows, and top risks
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Priya Rao <priya@synapselabs.ai>
Date: 2027-02-08T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: QBR compressed flightcheck: readiness, launch windows, and top risks

Team / Priya,

As promised, sending a short, focused snapshot ahead of Friday's QBR prep call. Goal: align product/eng status to the customer-facing launch timeline and highlight the three highest-impact delivery risks we need to close this week. Attachments: QBR_readiness_sheet_v2.xlsx (single-sheet status), launch-risk-heatmap.png.

High level:
- Roadmap: Feature set for the Synapse integration is feature-complete on paper; engineering has a known scope delta for two minor telemetry items (see sheet).
- Timeline: Target GA for Synapse integration: 2027-03-15. Recommended internal freeze: 2027-03-01 to maintain validation windows.
- Top risks (short):
  1) Model-serving perf under long-context customer traffic (owner: Jordan) — potential latency spike if KV caching not tuned.
  2) Migration window overlap with Synapse data migration (external coordination) — needs explicit 2-hour blackout window.
  3) E2E test flakiness triggered by rate-limited external endpoints (owner: Monica) — blocks validation.

Asks / decisions needed this week:
1) Jordan: confirm whether the new prefix-caching patch is deployable to staging by Thu (2027-02-11).
2) Monica: provide ETA on flaky E2E fixes and whether we can get an isolated run for Synapse by 2027-02-24.
3) Vivek: agree to the proposed internal freeze date (2027-03-01) or propose an alternate.
4) Priya: confirm acceptable migration blackout window and whether Synapse needs any additional evidence (SOC2 artifact) before the QBR.

I kept the sheet intentionally compact (one tab): status, owners, open risk mitigations. Please update the sheet inline where you add or change owners. I’ll consolidate into the QBR deck on Friday.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: QBR_readiness_sheet_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet)]
[Attachment: launch-risk-heatmap.png (image)]

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Priya Rao <priya@synapselabs.ai>
Date: 2027-02-09T10:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR compressed flightcheck: readiness, launch windows, and top risks

Ben — quick updates inline (edited the spreadsheet):

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team / Priya,
> 
> As promised, sending a short, focused snapshot ahead of Friday's QBR prep call. Goal: align product/eng status to the customer-facing launch timeline and highlight the three highest-impact delivery risks we need to close this week. Attachments: QBR_readiness_sheet_v2.xlsx (single-sheet status), launch-risk-heatmap.png.

Responses / changes:
- E2E flakiness: root cause looks like intermittent 429s from the telemetry ingestion endpoint when batch size > 128. I have a patch to retry/backoff that reduces failures locally; will run an isolated E2E on Wed 02/10 and a wider smoke on Thu 02/11. If that passes, we can lock owner \"E2E validation\" to Monica and mark mitigation in the sheet.
- Migration overlap: Synapse team has requested a 3-hour window (overnight) — Priya, can you confirm preferred start? We should avoid peak backup hours (US overnight).
- Test coverage gap: added one integration test for prefix-cache eviction; Jordan, please review my PR by EOD Wed so we can include it in the Thu staging run.

Risks with impact tiers (added to heatmap):
- Perf risk: medium-high (need Jordan's staging deploy to validate).
- Migration overlap: medium (customer coordination dependent).
- Compliance evidence: low (we can supply SOC2 artifact snapshot; may need redacted logs).

I also attached a short remediation playbook entry in the sheet for the 429 backoff pattern.

--
Monica Patel
Director, Platform Reliability | Redwood Inference
[Attachment references: updated QBR_readiness_sheet_v2.xlsx]


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Priya Rao <priya@synapselabs.ai>
Date: 2027-02-10T08:33:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR compressed flightcheck: readiness, launch windows, and top risks

Thanks for the snapshot. Two quick things:

1) I agree to the internal freeze of 2027-03-01 provided Jordan proves prefix-cache patch in staging by Thu. If staging looks good, we’ll keep GA at 2027-03-15.
2) Contingency: prepare a fallback variant with quantized model (lower perf but higher throughput) that we can flip within 30 minutes if we see production latency regressions during the migration window. Jordan/Monica, can you scope the fallback variant work and list required rollback steps in the sheet by EOD Thu?

I’ll clear 30 mins on Thu for a quick engineering sync after the staging run.

—Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
VP, Engineering | Redwood Inference


From: Priya Rao <priya@synapselabs.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-10T12:10:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR compressed flightcheck: readiness, launch windows, and top risks

Ben / team — thanks for the clear brief. A few notes from Synapse: 

- Migration window: we prefer 02:00–05:00 UTC on 2027-03-10 (overnight for us). If Redwood needs a narrower blackout, 02:30–04:30 UTC is acceptable.
- SOC2 evidence: we need a SOC2 Type II summary (redacted) and the ingestion retention policy for audit. Can you provide the SOC2 snapshot and an excerpt from your retention policy before the Friday QBR? A DocuSign link for the data access addendum would be helpful.
- Validation runs: Synapse can run parallel validation on 2027-02-25 if Redwood provides a staging endpoint and a short test manifest by 2027-02-22.

Attaching procurement-window-prop.docx with our preference and a short checklist.

Thanks,
Priya Rao
Head of Integrations | Synapse Labs
[Attachment: procurement-window-prop.docx (document)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Priya Rao <priya@synapselabs.ai>
Date: 2027-02-10T16:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR compressed flightcheck: readiness, launch windows, and top risks

Thanks everyone — consolidation below and next steps (short):

Decisions agreed:
- Internal freeze: 2027-03-01 (Vivek confirmed contingent on Thu staging pass).
- Customer migration window: Tentative 2027-03-10 02:30–04:30 UTC (Priya prefers 02:00–05:00; we will propose 02:30–04:30).

Action items (owner / due):
- Jordan Blake: complete prefix-cache patch review and deploy to staging by 2027-02-11. If staging pass, document rollback and fallback variant scope by 2027-02-11 EOD.
- Monica Patel: complete E2E isolated run on 2027-02-10 and wider smoke on 2027-02-11; update sheet with remediation notes and acceptance criteria.
- Vivek Kulkarni: confirm fallback variant window and sign off on freeze by 2027-02-11 morning.
- Ben Carter: provide SOC2 snapshot and retention excerpt to Priya by 2027-02-12 and add DocuSign link for the data access addendum.

I’ll prepare a two-slide exec snapshot for Friday’s QBR prep meeting capturing the three risks, owners, and the migration window ask for the customer-facing deck. If anything changes in staging runs, flag here immediately so I can adjust the deck.

Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
[Attachment references: consolidated QBR slide stub will be created from QBR_readiness_sheet_v2.xlsx]

title: Post-call: Technical fit & buying criteria for EpiHealth LLM exit
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Carter <sam.carter@epihealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 8 Feb 2027 16:03:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: Technical fit & buying criteria for EpiHealth LLM exit

Hi Sam — thanks again for taking time today. Quick written recap from our call so we have alignment and clear next steps.

Key pain points you shared:
- Vendor lock-in with current proprietary APIs (OpenAI + Anthropic) — need a migration path that preserves behavior and minimizes engineering lift.
- Cost unpredictability at scale (embedding + chat token spend).
- Latency spikes and inconsistent 95th pct performance for interactive clinical workflows.
- Data governance: PHI handling, audit trail, and KMS integration.

Current stack (what we heard):
- Product: conversational clinical assistant + semantic search for notes.
- Models in use: OpenAI Chat (gpt-4.1) for generation, Anthropic Claude for sensitive prompts, OpenAI embeddings for search.
- Infra: calls run from AWS us-east-1; some on-prem ETL for de-identification.

Success criteria for you (explicit):
1) Functional parity on key prompts (answer quality within existing tolerance).
2) 95th percentile latency <= 200ms for 1–2k token workloads in production routing.
3) Unit cost reduction target: ~30% per interactive session (embeddings + generation).
4) Security and compliance: SOC2 + BAA + KMS + audit logs for token retention.
5) Migration risk: ability to fallback to proprietary provider for 2–4 weeks during cutover.

Model shortlist we discussed as candidates for the POC (motion: evaluate open variants for parity + quantized runtimes):
- Llama 2 (13B / 70B) tuned variants
- Mistral-Instruct (7B) for cost-sensitive paths
- Falcon-40B Instruct (select prompts)
(we'll validate prompt-level parity and cost/latency tradeoffs in the POC)

Proposed next steps (timeline & owners):
- Redwood: deliver a one-page POC plan + cost estimate (Ben) — by Wed 2/10. Attachment: redwood-mini-proposal.pdf
- Redwood Solutions: share technical POC checklist + infra requirements (Marissa) — by Wed 2/10. Attachment: epihealth-poc-checklist.xlsx
- EpiHealth: confirm security contacts and BAA/POC sign-off (Sam) — reply to this thread with names
- Legal/Procurement: exchange NDA (DocuSign link) and outline procurement milestones (Ben)

Open questions we promised to follow up on:
- Any hard constraints on token retention windows for audit (you mentioned 30–90 days?)
- Are there prompts that must never leave your VPC (PHI)? We should mark them for private-deploy only.
- Preferred success metrics for quality (exact prompts + NPS/acceptance targets).

If that aligns, we'll send the POC plan + pricing. Happy to jump on a quick 15m tomorrow to finalize the checklist.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0138

---
Attachment(s): redwood-mini-proposal.pdf (proposal, 2pg), epihealth-poc-checklist.xlsx (POC tasks)

From: Sam Carter <sam.carter@epihealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call: Technical fit & buying criteria for EpiHealth LLM exit

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. Good summary; a couple clarifications from my side and a few asks before we sign off on a POC: 

Clarifications / adds:
- PHI: yes, anything with direct identifiers must either be de-identified before hitting any external service or run inside our VPC with a private deployment.
- Retention: target 90 days for audit logs tied to clinical decisions, but we can be pragmatic for ephemeral contexts.

POC asks / acceptance gates:
1) Demonstrate parity on 5 canonical clinical prompts (we'll share these).
2) Technical runbook showing how to rollback to current provider under 30 minutes.
3) High-level TCO estimate for a 6-month pilot at the expected volume (we estimate ~1.2M tokens/month).

Security wants to be looped in — please include security@epihealth.com on any compliance docs. Also we will need a BAA before any PHI sees Redwood infra.

If you can get the POC plan + checklist and a ballpark cost by Wed, we'll sync internally and aim to get procurement alignment next week. Looking to start a POC mid-Feb if all checks out.

Sam Carter
VP Product, EpiHealth Inc.
sam.carter@epihealth.com | +1 (617) 555-0199

---
Quoted original: "Key pain points you shared..."

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Carter <sam.carter@epihealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 10:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call: Technical fit & buying criteria for EpiHealth LLM exit

Sam — appreciated the quick reply. Adding a few concrete items from Solutions/Delivery to keep momentum: 

1) POC scope (high level) — proposed by Redwood Solutions (target 4 weeks):
   - Week 0: Kickoff, NDA & security intake (gather SOC2/BAA requirements)
   - Week 1: Ingest 5 canonical prompts + dataset slices; baseline quality against current provider
   - Week 2: Deploy 2 model candidates (quantized runtimes) and run latency/cost benchmarks
   - Week 3: Integrate fallback routing + runhouse tests; produce runbook for rollback
   - Week 4: Result summary, decision criteria review, next-phase proposal

2) Resources we'll assign: 1 Solutions Engineer (Grace O'Connor) + 1 Delivery PM + account exec (Ben)

3) Comms & approvals: please share security@epihealth.com contact and the internal stakeholder who will approve the pilot. We'll route the BAA draft (nda-epihealth-draft.docx) via DocuSign; pushing link now.

4) Ballpark pricing for pilot (estimate): $18k–$28k for a 4-week pilot depending on model selection and private-hosting needs. We'll firm this up in the mini-proposal.

I'll attach the POC checklist and the mini-proposal. If that looks OK we'll send a DocuSign NDA and propose a kickoff slot (Mon 2/15 or Tue 2/16).

Thanks — Marissa
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0222

---
Attachment(s): epihealth-poc-checklist.xlsx, nda-epihealth-draft.docx

title: ClearWave 8-week POC — success metrics, owners & next steps
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>, Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 Jan 2027 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Hi Sanjay and Claire,

Thanks again for a productive demo today. As promised, here’s a short recap and a proposed set of success criteria we can use to evaluate the 8-week POC. I focused on the areas you raised: latency, throughput, cost, and quality.

High-level asks / next steps:
- Confirm the sample dataset we should use (you mentioned ~25k anonymized queries).
- Sign the attached SOW so we can schedule reserved capacity for week 1.
- Confirm the stakeholder who will own evaluation and acceptance (QC/ML lead).

Proposed POC success KPIs (draft):
1) Latency: p95 generation time <= 220ms for 1–2k token prompts under our streamed response mode.
2) Throughput: baseline sustained 40 requests/sec with 80 concurrent KV-cache-backed sessions during busiest window.
3) Cost: average model cost <= $0.0007 per token (we’ll report token consumption and unit cost daily).
4) Quality: human-evaluated relevance >= 88% on your annotated 1k holdout set (sample rubric attached); automated BLEU/ROUGE comparisons for regression checks.
5) Reliability: <1% model error rate and endpoint availability >= 99.5% during the trial window.

Ownership (suggested):
- Redwood infra & perf: Marcus Lin (will own workload shaping, batching, and region routing)
- Evaluation & data: Rachel Kim (will coordinate test harness and human evaluation)
- Commercial & escalation: Ava Chen (single point of contact for delivery & contract items)

Tentative timeline (we can adjust after you confirm dataset and SOW):
- Week 0: SOW sign & data exchange (we provision dedicated pool)
- Week 1: Smoke tests + baseline profiling
- Week 2-3: Iteration on prompts & quantization profile; run throughput tests
- Week 4: Mid-POC checkpoint (metrics review; adjust acceptance thresholds if needed)
- Week 6-8: Final evaluation & acceptance decision

Attachments: ClearWave_POC_SOW.pdf, clearwave_sample_payloads.zip (sample queries + annotation guide)

Please confirm if these KPIs match what you discussed internally and who will be the acceptance owner on your side. I can push a signed SOW back today if the commercial terms look good.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198


From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Feb 2027 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks Ava — this is great and aligns with our expectations. A few clarifications from our side:
- Dataset: we’ll provide 30k anonymized user queries plus a 1k holdout labeled set by Tue (2/2).
- Acceptance owner: Claire will be primary for the quality pass; she wants to be looped on the daily automated reports.
- Latency bar: Claire asked if p95 can be targeted to 200ms instead of 220ms — how much headroom do you expect?

Action: I’ll review the SOW with procurement; if terms are unchanged we can sign by Tue EOD.

—Sanjay
Sanjay Patel
Head of Product
ClearWave Analytics


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Feb 2027 11:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Quick notes on feasibility:
- 200ms p95 is achievable for many of our LLM variants with streaming enabled, assuming avg prompt length stays under ~800 tokens and we route to an NVLink-backed pool. If they push longer prompts, latency jumps.
- Throughput: 40 req/sec sustained with 80 concurrent sessions is fine if we reserve a 4-GPU pool with continuous batching. Will coordinate with capacity team once SOW is signed.
- Cost: I can produce an expected cost/token table for two model classes (standard / low-cost quantized) by EOD Tue.

I’ll prep a short runbook for Claire that explains how we measure p95 and how to reproduce throughput tests.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Senior Infra Eng, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>, Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Feb 2027 09:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks Sanjay — that plan works. Claire, great to hear you’ll own the quality pass. A couple of operational notes:
- We measure p95 at the gateway after batching and just before byte encoding to reflect application latency. I’ll include the exact curl and k6 commands in the runbook.
- For quality: Rachel, can you confirm the rubric columns you need on the 1k holdout? I propose: Relevance (0–3), Factuality (0–1), and Response Appropriateness (0–2).

Next steps I’ll do once I get your go-ahead:
1) Send SOW for signature (DocuSign link)
2) Marcus will provision a 4-GPU reserved pool and post the capacity booking ETA
3) Rachel will get the evaluation harness spun up and share the annotation sheet by Wed EOD

Ava


From: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 03 Feb 2027 13:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks — that rubric looks good. Couple of tweaks from QC:
- Relevance threshold: we’ll require >= 88% where relevance is 2 or 3 on your 0–3 scale.
- Factuality: we’ll flag any hallucinations; acceptable threshold is <= 3% hallucination rate on the holdout.

We’ll send the annotated 1k by EOD Thu (2/4). We can live with the 220ms p95 if Redwood can show a clear path to 200ms by the final evaluation (we need to justify this to our product leadership).

Claire Johnson
Head of Data Science
ClearWave Analytics


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Feb 2027 09:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Perfect — thanks for the rubric and timeline. I’ve attached the DocuSign SOW (link below).
DocuSign: https://docusign.com/link/rw-clearwave-7421

I’ll have Marcus send the capacity confirmation once provisioned and Rachel will share the evaluation sheet today. Looking forward to kick-off next week.

Best,
Ava

title: MAP: GPU split-test & multipass perf results + close timeline
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 10 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP draft: perf gating + hardware split-test proposal

Samantha — attaching our proposed Mutual Action Plan (high level) and the GPU split-test design so we can align on success gates. Key asks: sign off on the MAP milestones below and grant access for our evaluation harness to Polaris staging workload for 48 hours starting Jan 18.

High-level MAP (milestones):
- Jan 12: Confirm evaluation seeds (prompts + sample traffic) — Polaris
- Jan 18–19: Redwood executes split-test (A100 / H100 / L40S + MIG variants) on Polaris staging traffic
- Jan 20: Provide raw perf CSV + condensed results to Polaris
- Jan 22: Joint review and recommended fleet sizing + commercial proposal
- Jan 25: Finalize contract signature / reserved capacity start date

Perf success gates (we propose):
- TTFT (time-to-first-token) <= 70ms for single-stream unbatched at target model size
- p95 latency <= 200ms for 512-token responses at target concurrency
- sustained throughput >= 60k tokens/sec for 1-min steady-state on production mix
- graceful degradation (fallback to cheaper variant < 5% error rate)

Split-test design: run identical request stream with 1) H100 full, 2) A100 full, 3) L40S full, and 4) representative MIG slices (1g/2g equivalent). Each run: 2 warm-up minutes + 30 minutes steady-state; repeated twice. Instrument: p50/p95/p99 latency, TTFT, tokens/sec, GPU utilization, memory tail behavior, KV cache hit rates.

Attachments: polaris_map_v1.pdf (MAP), h100_a100_l40s_split_results.csv (template), ttft_concurrency_notes.pdf (test recipe)

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 11:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP draft: perf gating + hardware split-test proposal

Agree with the gates; one add: include a cold-start TTFT measurement after a 30-min idle window to simulate on-demand infrequent flows. Also propose adding a recommended cost-per-token delta in the results table to help procurement.

Quick checklist for the run engineers:
- Ensure KV cache priming scripts run prior to each steady-state window
- Use same model checkpoint across HW types (quantization profile applied consistently)
- Capture kernel selection logs for H100/A100 (useful for regressions)

I'll slot a canary in Production for Feb 1 if we clear the MAP by Jan 25.

- Karthik


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@cloudops.io>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jan 2027 08:32:00 -0800
Subject: Polaris staging access + eval harness instructions (Jan 18–19)

Samantha — thanks for the quick sync. Per our call:
- We've provisioned the Redwood eval harness and need a service account with read-only streaming access to Polaris staging Canary API.
- Start window: 2027-01-18 10:00 PST; duration: 48 hours, but we will only perform the split-test during the 10:00–14:00 PST window on Jan 18 and repeat Jan 19 if needed.

What to expect in the payload:
- 60/40 user:system distribution; avg request length ~ 420 tokens; 15% long-form (>1024 tokens)
- We will pin the same model (r-infer-2.1) and run the kernel selection for each HW type; quantization: 4-bit canonical profile.

Please upload the service-account key to the Drive folder and let us know when the staging permissions are live. We'll stream logs and attach the CSV results to the shared Drive link.

Aditya


From: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@cloudops.io>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 15:55:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Polaris staging access + eval harness instructions (Jan 18–19)

Team — staging key uploaded and access granted. We ran the first pass this morning; preliminary observations attached (short-form). A request: can Redwood include a MIG 3g slice in the second pass? Our ops team is considering both full-card and MIG economics.

Attached: polaris_staging_prelim_notes.txt (short), see Drive.

— Samantha


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Jan 2027 09:04:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: split-test condensed results + sizing recommendation (draft)

Team — pulling together the condensed results from the Jan 18 run (attached CSV). Top-line (steady-state windows, warmed KV cache):
- H100 (full): p50=7.8ms, p95=21.7ms, p99=45ms; avg throughput=122k tokens/sec; TTFT (single-stream, unbatched)=48ms
- L40S (full): p50=9.9ms, p95=31.2ms, p99=68ms; avg throughput=103k tokens/sec; TTFT=62ms
- A100 (full): p50=12.4ms, p95=39.8ms, p99=85ms; avg throughput=89k tokens/sec; TTFT=81ms
- MIG 3g slice (on H100): p50=18.6ms, p95=46.0ms, p99=110ms; avg throughput=38k tokens/sec; TTFT=95ms

Notes and interpretation:
- H100 wins on raw latency and throughput; L40S is close and offers a better price/throughput ratio for Polaris's mixed workload. A100 lags on p95/p99 and TTFT, which impacts interactive UX for short-turn flows.
- MIG slices are predictable for low-cost isolation but reduce throughput significantly; useful for small isolated tenant workloads.

Sizing recommendation (Polaris target: 300k tokens/sec steady-state + 200 concurrent interactive users):
- Preferred fleet: 6 x L40S (full) + 2 x H100 (full) for burst and low-latency tail coverage. This yields an expected steady throughput ~640k tokens/sec peak with headroom for autoscaling.
- Alternate cost-first fleet: 10 x L40S (full) and use MIG 3g slices for tenant isolation where needed.

Commercial delta: incremental cost per 1M tokens served (approx): H100 baseline: $11.2; L40S: $8.9; A100: $10.5 (approx; final quote in proposal).

Action items for MAP closure:
- Jan 22: Internal sign-off on sizing (this thread)
- Jan 24: Send draft commercial proposal to Polaris (Laura to lead)
- Jan 25: Final negotiation and DocuSign signatures

Ben
Senior Pricing Analyst


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Jan 2027 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Final MAP check-in: recommended fleet & next steps to close

Samantha — thanks for the partnership during the split-test. Summary and next steps: 

Summary (agreed):
- Performance: H100 best raw perf; L40S nearly as fast for steady workloads and presents the best cost/throughput trade-off for Polaris's workload mix. MIG slices are viable for tenant isolation but not for mainline throughput.
- Sizing: Recommended production fleet = 6 x L40S + 2 x H100; alternate cost-first proposal included in the draft commercial.
- Gates met: TTFT median and p95 targets met on warmed KV cache; cold-start TTFT failed the 70ms gate on A100 but passed on H100/L40S.

Next steps (timeline):
- Jan 25: Redwood sends final commercial (Laura) + DocuSign (done, link above)
- Jan 27: Polaris countersign (target)
- Feb 1: Redwood canary (small pct of traffic) if signatures in place
- Feb 7: Full capacity ramp on reserved fleet

Samantha — please confirm the counter-sign target and whether Polaris wants the alternative cost-first configuration included in the SOW. Once we have signatures we will schedule the Feb 1 canary and provide runbooks for rollback and autoscale thresholds.

Thanks everyone — moving to close.

Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

title: Phase 1 Onboarding Sprint Charter — Horizon Health
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@horizonhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 09:07:00 -0800
Subject: Phase 1 Onboarding Sprint Charter — Horizon Health

Hi Samira,

Thanks again for the productive demo last week. Attached is our first-pass Phase 1 sprint charter (Horizon_Phase1_MAP_v2.xlsx) that lays out a tightly scoped 8-week delivery plan focused on integrating Redwood inference for Horizon’s triage assistant use case. Core objectives here are: enable SSO, validate data mappings, and complete a safe canary to production.

Key asks from you to confirm so we can lock dates:
- Procurement / PO target date (if already planned)
- Primary tech contact for API keys and firewall whitelisting (I have Aaron Lee listed)
- Any blackout windows for Jan–Mar where dev access won’t be allowed

High-level milestones (summary — full table in attachment):
- Week 0 (kickoff): SSO & security intake, scope finalization
- Week 1–2: Data mapping + integration sandbox
- Week 3: End-to-end integration + basic acceptance tests
- Week 5: Canary with limited traffic (1%–5%)
- Week 8: Production go-live + knowledge transfer

I copied Monica (CS lead) and Marissa (Implementation PM) — they’ll own day-to-day scheduling and the acceptance checklist. If that owner split looks good to you, Monica will send a weekly cadence invite and Marissa will publish the milestone tracker.

Attachments: Horizon_Phase1_MAP_v2.xlsx (MAP table + roles), integration_requirements.pdf (API and firewall checklist).

Proposed kickoff: Thu Jan 21, 10:00–10:45 PT — want to confirm?

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 415-555-0123

--

Attachment: integration_requirements.pdf (pdf, 6 pages)

Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Phase 1 Onboarding Sprint Charter — Horizon Health

Ben — thanks for the overview and for pulling this together. The plan looks pragmatic and scoped appropriately for Phase 1. A couple quick items from our side:

- Procurement: PO is in flight; procurement expects to issue by Jan 27. I’ll confirm if you need any special invoice fields.
- Tech contact: Aaron Lee will be the primary for firewall/keys (cc’d). He’ll also coordinate with our identity team for SSO.
- Blackout windows: We have a scheduled maintenance on Feb 14–15 (plan for no deploys those dates).

Kickoff on Thu Jan 21 at 10:00 PT works for us. Please include a 15-minute pre-kick call for Aaron + our security engineer so we can confirm firewall/IP ranges in advance.

We will also need to complete a brief security checklist — can you confirm who will own providing evidence for:
- SOC 2 report
- Data retention policy & logs
- Customer audit contact

Thanks — looking forward to getting started.

Samira Khan
Director, Product Operations
Horizon Health
samira.khan@horizonhealth.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@horizonhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Aaron Lee <aaron.lee@horizonhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Jan 2027 08:41:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Phase 1 Onboarding Sprint Charter — Horizon Health

Samira — appreciated. Quick confirmations and the sprint charter with owners filled out (see attached spreadsheet v2):

Assigned owners (Phase 1):
- CS Lead / Adoption: Monica Patel (monica.patel@redwoodinference.com) — weekly cadence, acceptance checklist, customer training
- Implementation PM: Marissa Cole (marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com) — milestone tracker, runbooks, cutover windows
- Tech Integration: Aaron Lee (aaron.lee@horizonhealth.com) — firewall/SSO/API ops
- Account Exec: Ben Carter — procurement sync, contract-level items, escalation

Detailed milestone table (extracted from attachment):
- Kickoff (Jan 21): kickoff + security pre-call (owner: Ben / Aaron) — deliverable: signed MAP, kickoff notes
- SSO & Security Intake (Jan 21–27): configure SAML test, collect SOC2 + retention docs (owner: Aaron / Monica) — deliverable: verification checklist
- Sandbox Integration (Jan 28–Feb 10): mapping of payloads, schema validation, initial API calls (owner: Aaron / Marissa) — deliverable: sandbox success runbook
- E2E Validation (Feb 11–18): acceptance tests across NLU flows (owner: Marissa) — deliverable: test report; note blackout Feb 14–15
- Canary (Mar 1–7): limited traffic canary, monitor latency & error rates, rollback plan ready (owner: Monica) — deliverable: canary sign-off
- Production Go-live (Mar 15): full cutover and KT sessions (owner: Monica / Marissa) — deliverable: production acceptance & runbook handoff

Acceptance criteria (examples):
- Response latency P95 < 600ms for 512 tokens in canary traffic
- Token cost estimates within 15% of forecast for the selected quantization profile
- No security exceptions in SSO or firewall checks

Risk register (top-3):
1) SSO delays (mitigation: parallel API key-based sandbox)
2) Schema mismatches for payloads (mitigation: sample dataset mapping session week 1)
3) Unexpected latency under peak (mitigation: canary thresholds + immediate fallback to smaller model variant)

Next steps:
- Monica to send weekly meeting invite (Wed cadence) and a short acceptance checklist template by EOD Tue.
- Aaron to confirm firewall IPs and send SAML metadata ahead of the kickoff.
- Ben to loop procurement with any invoice instructions once Samira confirms PO fields.

I attached an updated Horizon_Phase1_MAP_v2.xlsx with specific dates and owners, and integration_requirements.pdf with the API call examples.

Thanks — see you Thu for kickoff.

Ben

--

Attachment: integration_requirements.pdf

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>

Samira — great to meet you (virtually) in the kickoff. Quick follow-up with the operational plan and what we’ll deliver each week — this is the working cadence we’ll rely on to measure progress: 

Operational cadence (week-by-week snapshot):
- Week 0 (Jan 21): Kickoff notes + security pre-call completed; SAML test scheduled
- Week 1 (Jan 22–28): Endpoint & schema mapping; sample payload run; acceptance checklist draft
- Week 2 (Jan 29–Feb 4): Full sandbox integration; first 50 automated tests run by Marissa
- Week 3 (Feb 5–11): E2E scenario tests with Horizon QA; sign-off gating criteria reviewed
- Week 4 (Feb 12–18): Remediation window; freeze during Feb 14–15; prepare canary metrics dashboard
- Week 5–6 (Mar 1–7): Canary execution and monitoring; roll-forward or rollback decision point
- Week 8 (Mar 15): Production handoff + customer enablement session

Deliverables I’ll own and circulate each Wed: sprint health one-pager, acceptance checklist status, risk updates, and canary gate decision template. I’ll link the tracker to the Drive doc (see hubspot/drive link above).

A few asks for Horizon before the sandbox work begins:
1) A small sample dataset (anonymized) for schema validation — format: JSONL, ~200 records
2) SAML metadata XML or Okta tenant details
3) Contact for on-call during canary week (name + phone)

If you can confirm those by Friday Jan 22, we’ll be in a strong position to start sandbox tests on Jan 28.

title: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review
preview:
From: Lydia Chen <lydia.chen@devbank.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: DevBank Security Team <ops-gov@devbank.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. As part of our SIG/CAIQ review we need a concise resilience pack focused on your BCP/DR posture — specifically:

- Current RTO and RPO commitments for the hosted API and Dedicated deployments (numbers and how they differ by tier).
- Evidence of most recent failover / restore tests (summary of test scope, date, outcomes).
- Any scheduled warm-cache / KV warm-up timings and measured recovery times.
- The runbook or playbook references you use for region failover.

If possible, please provide a 1–2 page summary PDF and the raw test logs or CSVs for the last full DR rehearsal. We have a tight internal review window and would like these by end of day Tuesday (local).

Appreciate it — Lydia

Lydia Chen
Senior InfoSec Manager
DevBank


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Jan 2027 10:03:00 -0500
Subject: Fwd: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review

Team — forwarding DevBank's ask (below). They want a short resilience pack + raw logs. Lydia gave a hard deadline (EOD Tuesday). Can we pull together:

- Marissa: 1–2 page summary for SIG/CAIQ context with RTO/RPO table and high-level test outcomes.
- Marcus: attach the last full DR rehearsal logs (CSV) and quick note on cache warm-up times; add any caveats re: Dedicated vs Hosted.
- Hannah: confirm whether the region failover runbook referenced is the public runbook or an internal variant and whether redaction is needed.

I'll collate and send. If we can't meet Tuesday, propose a short extension and send what we have.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 08:45:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review

Hi all — attaching the requested summary and the SIG section draft. Quick notes:

- RTO/RPO: Hosted API SLA target: RTO=30 minutes (limited scope for control-plane failover), RPO=15 minutes (best-effort for in-flight requests); Dedicated (reserved) pools: RTO=15 minutes for node replacement/fallback, RPO=5 minutes with KV prefix caching.
- Last full DR rehearsal: 2026-10-12 — primary->secondary failover across us-east/us-west for control plane; attached CSV is the post-run telemetry. Recovery times measured are in the CSV; summary in the PDF.
- KV warming: automated prefix-cache restore takes ~120–180s for prioritized prefixes; full KV ramp to steady-state depends on workload and may be up to 20 minutes. We call out mitigation (prefetch heuristics + sequential ramp).
- Runbook: we will provide a redacted runbook link (internal sections removed).

Attachments: rto_rpo_summary.pdf, recovery_test_log_20261012.csv, sig_section_BCP_responses.docx

Marissa Cole
Head of Compliance, Redwood Inference


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Lydia Chen <lydia.chen@devbank.com>, DevBank Security Team <ops-gov@devbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 14:05:00 -0500
Subject: Resilience pack and DR rehearsal logs — Redwood -> DevBank

Hi Lydia / DevBank team,

Thanks for the request — please find our resilience pack attached and the raw rehearsal log for your reviewers. Highlights:

- rto_rpo_summary.pdf: 2-page executive summary with RTO/RPO commitments, test summary, and mitigation controls.
- recovery_test_log_20261012.csv: raw timestamps from our Oct 12, 2026 rehearsal (primary->secondary regional failover).
- sig_section_BCP_responses.docx: draft answers keyed to SIG/CAIQ BCP questions; please treat as draft during review.

Accessible resources: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcResiliencePack (contains an additional redacted runbook excerpt and the SRE post-mortem). Ticket for follow-up and tracking: JIRA INF-4821.

If you need any clarifications on the CSV columns or want the SRE team to walk through the reheasal telemetry with your reviewers, Marcus is happy to join a short call.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

title: RFI: Implementation plan & gated rollout questionnaire — Orchid Financial
preview:
From: Elena Soto <elena.soto@orchidfinancial.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 08:14:00 -08:00
Subject: RFI: Implementation plan & canary gating — Orchid Financial

Hi Ben,

Thanks for your submission so far. As part of our RFI for vendor selection we need a concise implementation/migration questionnaire that covers timeline, integration touchpoints, canary/rollout gating criteria, rollback plans, and operational runbooks. The procurement team would like:

- A staged timeline for a pilot -> production migration (including typical durations and dependencies)
- Integration steps for our auth/data integrations (SAML, SCIM, VPC peering, audit logs)
- Canary gating rules (metrics, thresholds, duration) and fallback behaviors
- Rollback/runbook examples and who we expect to interact with on each step
- Any constraints for air-gapped environments or data residency controls

Can you provide a single consolidated document (or set of artifacts) addressing the above by Jan 18? We will share this with our security and platform teams.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Director, Vendor Strategy
Orchid Financial

-----Original Message-----



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@orchidfinancial.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: RFI: Implementation plan & canary gating — Orchid Financial

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear ask. We can absolutely provide a consolidated implementation questionnaire and artifacts by Jan 18.

High-level plan:
- Draft consolidated responses in a single PDF plus an appendix workbook (timeline, gating matrix).
- Monica will own the integration details (SAML, SCIM, VPC, audit exports).
- Laura will validate the runbook, canary gating thresholds and rollback windows.
- I'll consolidate and send to you with requested attachments and a short executive summary.

I've attached an initial draft outline (Orchid_RFI_Implementation_Responses.pdf) with placeholders. Monica/Laura: please review the integration steps and runbook sections inline and update the appendix spreadsheet with estimated durations.

Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0128

Attachments: Orchid_RFI_Implementation_Responses.pdf (pdf)


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Jan 2027 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Internal: Draft integration steps and staging assumptions (ORCH RFI)

Ben/Laura — internal draft below (will not send to Orchid until we consolidate).

Integration steps (high level):
1) Authentication and user provisioning
   - SAML2.0: Orchid will provide IdP metadata and an OIDC/SAML test account. Typical turnaround: 3-5 business days for initial SSO validation.
   - SCIM: optional for provisioning. If enabled, we expect SCIM v2 endpoints from Orchid; typical work: 2-4 dev days for connector mapping and 1 test cycle.

2) Network and data plane
   - VPC peering (hosted mode) or private endpoints for Private deployments. Network team will need CIDR ranges and peering windows. Typical time: 5-10 business days for peering and validated throughput tests.
   - For air-gapped/on-prem we provide a packaging checklist; expected timeline depends on customer infra (estimate 6-8 weeks end-to-end for on-prem).

3) Audit and telemetry
   - Audit logs via S3 push / syslog or API pull. Retention and redaction rules configurable. Provide sample schema and test payload within 2 business days of request.

Gated rollout and canary proposal (draft):
- Canary stage 1: Internal sandbox validation (0% customer traffic) — smoke tests and load sanity. Duration: 24–48 hours.
- Canary stage 2: 5% production traffic, 72 hours, monitor latency P95, error rate, and application-level correctness (LLM hallucination/regression checks). Thresholds (subject to tuning): P95 latency increase <= 15% vs baseline; error rate <= 0.5% absolute increase; inference quality regression <= 2% on sample prompt set.
- Canary stage 3: 20% production traffic, 48 hours — expanded performance checks and resource scaling validation.
- Full rollout: 100% after green across stages and sign-off by platform owner.

Rollback and fallback:
- Automatic fallback to last known-good model variant (compatible family) if error rate breaches thresholds or sustained latency > threshold for > 10 minutes.
- Manual rollback path: IP-based drain, revert routing, and model pin to prior version with validated KV cache flush instructions.

Runbook owners:
- Engineering on-call (Redwood): runbook step executor for quick rollbacks.
- Platform owner (Orchid): approves production gating progression and final cutover.

I'll populate the Appendix matrix with durations and a preliminary gating matrix (attached as Appendix-G-Rollout-Matrix.xlsx).

—Monica
Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: Appendix-G-Rollout-Matrix.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Jan 2027 10:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Internal: Draft integration steps and staging assumptions (ORCH RFI)

Thanks — looks good. A few suggested edits before external send:

1) Make the canary thresholds explicit and add a human approval gate after each canary (Platform owner + Redwood SRE).
2) For the 5% canary, call out specific metrics we will track:
   - Request success rate (200-level responses)
   - Model output agreement on a 100-prompt seed set (we'll include a sample eval report)
   - Cost throttle behavior during burst — ensure autoscaling constraints are observed.
3) Add an explicit communication cadence for cutover weekends (pre-cutover checklist 48 hours prior; daily standups during 72 hour windows).

Also recommend a default canary duration of 72 hours at 5% to capture longer-tail failures seen in caching-heavy workloads. For on-prem/air-gapped, include a separate checkboxed runbook for KMS/HSM key rotation and validation.

I'll mark the runbook "ready for external" once Monica updates the appendix.

—Laura Bennett
Program Manager, Customer Deployments
laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@orchidfinancial.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Orchid Financial Procurement <procurement@orchidfinancial.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Jan 2027 13:08:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: RFI: Implementation plan & gated rollout questionnaire — Orchid Financial

Hi Elena / Orchid team — please find attached our consolidated responses to the implementation portion of the RFI. The package includes:

- Orchid_RFI_Implementation_Responses.pdf — executive summary and answers to your questionnaire.
- Appendix-G-Rollout-Matrix.xlsx — stage durations, owner responsibilities, and gating thresholds.
- Orchid_Canary_Runbook.md — step-by-step runbook for canary deployment and rollback instructions.

Executive summary highlights (TL;DR):
- Pilot -> production typically spans 4–8 weeks depending on SCIM and VPC completion; hosted deployments often complete faster (2–3 weeks) when SAML-only.
- Recommended gated rollout: 5% for 72 hours -> 20% for 48 hours -> full cutover, with explicit human approval at each stage.
- Automatic fallback to a last-known-good model variant for immediate mitigation; manual rollback procedures documented in the runbook.
- Air-gapped/on-prem timelines materially longer (6–8 weeks) and require a separate packaging engagement.

Next steps: please confirm if you want a live review session (30–45 minutes) with our Delivery and SRE leads; we can walk the runbook and appendix.

Regards,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: Orchid_RFI_Implementation_Responses.pdf (pdf); Appendix-G-Rollout-Matrix.xlsx (xlsx); Orchid_Canary_Runbook.md (markdown)


From: Orchid Financial Procurement <procurement@orchidfinancial.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Soto <elena.soto@orchidfinancial.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 09:21:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: RFI: Implementation plan & gated rollout questionnaire — Orchid Financial

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Orchid Financial Procurement <procurement@orchidfinancial.com>, Elena Soto <elena.soto@orchidfinancial.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 14:06:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: RFI: Implementation plan & gated rollout questionnaire — Orchid Financial

Hi — quick answers inline below. Full artifacts attached where relevant.

1) Audit logs and encryption: Yes — we support server-side encryption with customer-managed keys (SSE-CMK) integrated with major KMS providers. For Private/on-prem we support HSM integrations and provide a KMS checklist in the appendix. You will find the KMS key rotation and attestations section in Orchid_RFI_Implementation_Responses.pdf (page 8).

2) Seed set and evaluation report: Attached is the sample 100-prompt seed set (seed_prompts.csv) and the evaluation report (seed_eval_report.html). Our methodology: multi-rater annotation on a 100-prompt representative set, automated metric of semantic overlap and a manual spot-check for hallucination scoring. We include the rubric and scorer notes in the report.

If you'd like, we can schedule a 30-minute walkthrough with our Applied ML team to talk methodology.

Thanks,
Ben

Attachments: seed_prompts.csv (csv); seed_eval_report.html (html)

title: OOMPH - Dedicated fleet sizing & next steps
preview:
From: Samantha Ruiz <samantha.ruiz@oomph.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jason Kim <jason.kim@oomph.ai>
Date: 2027-01-15T09:02:00Z
Subject: Dedicated fleet sizing questionnaire and traffic samples

Hey Ben —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Our engineering leadership wants a short, pragmatic sizing recommendation tied to the OOMPH proof-of-concept (POC). Attached is a small sample of production traffic and a one‑page Q&A our infra team filled out. High level asks from our side:

- A recommended GPU fleet (instance types + counts) for a 3-month POC, and projected steady-state for first-year growth.
- Estimated 95th‑pct p99 token latency and expected token cost per 1M tokens.
- Which model variants and batching/quantization tradeoffs you'd recommend for 500–1,000 token prompts and 1,000–2,500 token responses.

Quick clarifiers from the engineering team:
- We expect peak sustained concurrency of ~340 active requests during a 30-minute peak window (bursty across endpoints).
- Average prompt length: 480 tokens; average generated response: 1,150 tokens (but 20% of requests want longform ≥2,000 tokens).
- ~10% of calls will be embedding lookups (small payloads, high qps).

Can you and your solutions team produce a short sizing note + MLP (mutual action plan) by EOD tomorrow? We need it to land with procurement to finalize the POC budget.

Thanks,
Samantha
Samantha Ruiz
Head of ML, OOMPH AI


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Ruiz <samantha.ruiz@oomph.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-01-15T12:34:00Z
Subject: Re: Dedicated fleet sizing questionnaire and traffic samples

Samantha —

Got it. Thanks for the clear asks and the traffic samples — those are super helpful. Laura and Dev are looped for the technical sizing; I’ll own the commercial/MLP piece. Quick surface-level takeaways while we work the calc:

- With avg prompt ~480 and avg response ~1,150 tokens, effective tokens/request ≈1.63k tokens. At 340 concurrency during peaks, that implies ~554k tokens in-flight per second-window if served concurrently; we'll convert into GPU utilization across batching patterns.
- For the 20% longform (≥2k), we'll model a separate bucket with lower batching efficiency.
- Embeddings traffic (10%) usually maps better to CPU/embedding-optimized nodes or to separate embedding pools; we’ll recommend a small dedicated embedding fleet to avoid tail latency on generation nodes.

Laura / Dev — can you produce a 1‑page sizing note with recommended instance types, core fleet for POC (3 months) and a-year view, and two cost scenarios (conservative QoS, aggressive cost-optimized with quantization)? If possible include a short fallback plan: hosted smaller models for cold starts and canary routing.

I’ll draft the MLP (deliverables, owners, timeline, and procurement items) and attach the commercial outline for the POC.

Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Ruiz <samantha.ruiz@oomph.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-01-16T08:21:00Z
Subject: RE: Dedicated fleet sizing questionnaire and traffic samples

Ben / Samantha —

Attached is a concise sizing workbook (redwood_prelim-sizing.xlsx) and a short PDF with assumptions (oomph_expected_qps_breakdown.pdf). Highlights / assumptions in plain language:

- Workload buckets:
  • Interactive generation (primary): 80% of volume; average tokens/request 1.63k. 80% of those fit into standard batching windows; 20% longform treated as separate lane.
  • High‑qps embeddings: 10% of total calls; small payloads, high qps — recommend 2x embedding-optimized nodes for the POC.
  • System/housekeeping traffic: 10% (health checks, logging, retries).

- POC fleet recommendation (3 months):
  • 8 × A40-equivalent GPUs in a single dedicated pool for the Generation lane (target p99 token latency: 180–250ms with batching and KV caching).
  • 2 × T4-equivalent GPUs for embeddings (or a CPU + small GPU pool if cost-sensitive).
  • Config: 2 routing shards, continuous batching up to 64 seqs (or adaptive based on observed latency), KV prefix caching for top-10% hot prompts.

- Year-1 steady-state projection (12–18 months):
  • Base fleet 24 × A40-equivalents (autoscaling bursts to 36 during marketing events).
  • Embedding pool scaled to 6 × T4-equivalents.

- Model guidance:
  • Use a 7–13B variant for latency-sensitive interactive traffic with 4-bit quantization in the cost-optimized scenario; fallback to 13–30B for high-quality longform when latency budget allows (can be routed to separate higher-capacity nodes).

- Cost & performance scenarios included in the workbook: conservative (no quantization, 95th token cost higher) vs optimized (4-bit quant, adaptive batching). We included per‑1M token cost up-front and netted monthly run-rate for both POC and steady-state.

Next steps I recommend we align on:
1) OOMPH confirms target p99 token latency objective and absolute max tail latency they can tolerate.
2) Redwood to ship a validated MLP with milestones for capacity build, model pinning, security review, and procurement checklist.
3) Procurement to confirm billing contact and PO cadence so we can include firm cost line items.

Attachment stubs: redwood_prelim-sizing.xlsx (workbook with model-by-model sims), oomph_expected_qps_breakdown.pdf (assumptions + per-bucket QPS).

Laura Bennett
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

- A recommended GPU fleet (instance types + counts) for a 3-month POC, and projected steady-state for first-year growth.
- Estimated 95th‑pct p99 token latency and expected token cost per 1M tokens.

Quick clarifiers from the engineering team:
- We expect peak sustained concurrency of ~340 active requests during a 30-minute peak window (bursty across endpoints).

- With avg prompt ~480 and avg response ~1,150 tokens, effective tokens/request ≈1.63k tokens. At 340 concurrency during peaks, that implies ~554k tokens in-flight per second-window if served concurrently; we'll convert into GPU utilization across batching patterns.
- For the 20% longform (≥2k), we'll model a separate bucket with lower batching efficiency.
- Embeddings traffic (10%) usually maps better to CPU/embedding-optimized nodes or to separate embedding pools; we’ll recommend a small dedicated embedding fleet to avoid tail latency on generation nodes.

Laura / Dev — can you produce a 1‑page sizing note with recommended instance types, core fleet for POC (3 months) and a-year view, and two cost scenarios (conservative QoS, aggressive cost-optimized with quantization)? title: Post-call: LuminaHealth data handling alignment and pilot proposal
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nisha Rao <nisha.rao@luminahealth.ai>, Louise Cheng <louise.cheng@luminahealth.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Jan 2027 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Post-call: LuminaHealth data handling alignment and pilot proposal

Hi Nisha / Louise —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment on scope before legal/engineering dive:

Call recap (TL;DR)
- Primary ask: restrict persisted customer data and PII footprint from inference pipeline for pilot workloads.
- LuminaHealth priority: prove no long-term storage of raw patient prompts, auditability for redaction/pseudonymization, and option for EU-residency for model execution.
- Timeline: procurement looking to finish vendor selection by end of Feb; technical deep-dive requested next week.

What Redwood can offer for a pilot (high level)
- Option A: 72-hour ephemeral logs (all prompts & outputs retained for 72 hours, then auto-purged). Includes KV cache disabled for persistent writeback and strict access controls.
- Option B: KV-only retention (only model key-value caches retained up to 7 days; raw prompts never persisted). Requires limited feature parity for session recovery.
- Option C: Redaction + pseudonymization flow — we run input-side redaction rules client-side and accept pseudonymized tokens for inference; storage limited to pseudonymized traces for 30 days.

Attached: redwood_pilot-options.pdf (short one-pager comparing tradeoffs) and lumina_data-questions.xlsx (filled from today's call).

Immediate asks / next steps
1) Confirm which pilot option you want us to scope (A/B/C) — reply with preferred option and any hard constraints (e.g., must be EU-resident).
2) We’ll schedule a 60-min technical deep-dive with our infra team to validate PII flows and finalize the pilot checklist (proposed slots in calendar invite).
3) If legal needs an initial mapping, we can produce a data-flow diagram + SOC2 artifact packet ahead of the procurement review.

I’ll loop in Marissa for contracting and timeline.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0122

Attachment(s): redwood_pilot-options.pdf (1 page), lumina_data-questions.xlsx (3 sheets)


From: Nisha Rao <nisha.rao@luminahealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Louise Cheng <louise.cheng@luminahealth.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Jan 2027 14:40:10 -05:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: LuminaHealth data handling alignment and pilot proposal

Thanks Ben — helpful summary. A few clarifying questions from our side before we pick a path:

1) When you say "raw prompts never persisted" (Option B) — can you confirm whether any derivative artifacts (embedding vectors, internal model traces, debug logs) are written to disk or object storage beyond the in-memory/inference window? We need explicit confirmation to present to our privacy team.

2) For Option A (72-hour) — is there an immutable audit log of who accessed logs pre-purge? We need access logs for any retained window.

3) Our legal will ask for a mapping of PII fields from example payloads (PHI is a hard stop unless pseudonymized). Can Redwood provide a sample data-flow diagram and SOC2 evidence (control IDs around encryption & access reviews)?

4) On residency: we prefer EU execution for any PHI-proximal workloads. If pilot runs in EU, does the 72-hour purge still apply and how does key management differ?

Procurement timeline: if we get the deep-dive and a one-pager for legal by next Wednesday (20 Jan), we can complete vendor short-listing by 31 Jan.

Appreciate the quick follow-up — looping Louise (ops) and Marissa for next steps.

—Nisha
Nisha Rao
Head of Product, LuminaHealth
nisha.rao@luminahealth.ai | +44 20 7946 0211


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nisha Rao <nisha.rao@luminahealth.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jan 2027 09:15:25 -08:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: LuminaHealth data handling alignment and pilot proposal

Hi Nisha — quick note from Legal/Contracts side and proposed mitigations for PHI-sensitive scope:

- Immediate confirmation: Redwood does NOT route persisted raw prompt text to third-party analytics by default; persisted artifacts are limited to configured retention buckets in your tenant. We can restrict those buckets to KV-only or ephemeral windows per pilot.

- Derivative artifacts: embeddings and model-internal tensors are not persisted to object storage in normal config. Debug logs (if enabled) can be configured to redact PII or sent to your S3 with client-side encryption — we’ll flag that for the deep-dive.

- Auditability: For any retained window (e.g., 72-hour), we provide access logs (who/when) and an immutable event stream tie-able to the tenant. We can include log-export schedule in the pilot checklist.

- EU residency: we have EU execution regions and can sign residency addenda. Key management: we can use customer-managed keys (bring-your-own KMS) in EU regions — this is supported for Dedicated/Private deployments.

Attached is a short CSV that maps the pilot options to typical legal asks and evidence we can produce (retention-options.csv).

If acceptable, Ben — can you propose two slots for the tech deep-dive (prefer 90-min so eng can walk through data-flow and sample payloads)?

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Senior Contracts Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

Attachment(s): retention-options.csv (1 sheet, mapping)
